title: sambucus racemosa f. stenophylla
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. stenophylla, also known as dwarf or Rocky Mountain elderberry, is a shrubby or herbaceous plant species that belongs to the Sambucus genus, known for its medicinal properties. The leaves, bark, and berries of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to their antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa f. stenophylla include relief from symptoms of the common cold and flu, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
